tavily.answer: In 2025, the number of internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high, surpassing those caused by natural disasters for the first time, according to a report by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre. The United Nations refugee agency also reported that nearly 118 million people were displaced by conflict and persecution in the same year, marking the first decline in a decade. In the context of Tunisia, there is no specific data on international displacement to Tunisia due to conflict or security developments, but the global trend indicates a significant increase in internal displacements driven by conflict and violence.
